response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack and polyglot engineer, but the resume shows minimal alignment with the core requirements of this role. There is no demonstrated Kotlin experience, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate work, and only passing mentions of Java (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tool). The closest IDE experience is brief C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI a decade ago, which is not a JVM-based IDE. Primary strengths are in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — useful languages but not what this enterprise JVM tooling role requires.
strengths: Broad senior-level engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Past exposure to building developer tooling (LabWindows/CVI IDE components, MCP code generators, CI/CD infra)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, workshops on testing
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only superficial Java exposure on the resume
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate ecosystem experience listed
No substantive JVM internals or language-tooling work for Java/Kotlin IDEs

key_factors.factor: No JVM IDE internals or language tooling experience
Senior-level engineering breadth and tooling mindset
